title: Spectrophotometric determination of mercury(II) in environmental (water, soil and biological) samples using 3-methylthiophene-2-carbaxaldehyde thiosemicarbazone (3-MTAT)
author: D. Nagarjuna Reddy
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Sri Krishnadevaraya University, Anantapur-515 003, Andhra Pradesh, India 
 E-mail  : khussainreddy@yahoo.co.in 
 Manuscript received 01 February 2010, revised 10 August 2010, accepted 18 August 2010  

 Analytical applications of 3-methyllhiophene-:2-carbaxaldehyde thiosemicarbazone (3-MTAT) are reported for the first time. The reagent has been synthesized and characterized using IR, NMR and Mass spectral data. 3-MTAT have been used for the spectrophotometric determination of mercury(ll). The reagent reacts with mercury(ll) in basic medium (pH 8.0, ammonium chloride and ammonium buffer) to form yellow colored 1: :2 (M : L) complex. The color reaction is instantaneous and absorbance values remain constant for l:Z h. The molar absorptivity and Sandell's sensitivity of 3-MTAT method are found to be 1.87 x 10 4  L mol -1  cm -1  and 0.0107 μg cm -2  of mercury(ll) respectively. The method Is successfully applied to a number of water (potable and polluted), biological and soil samples containing mercury(II). The results of the proposed method in the analysis of samples are comparable with those obtained by dithizone method. The method bas high accuracy and precision (s = 0.0042 for 4 μg/ml in 3-MTAT method).
